risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The randomisation list was generated by a statistician independent of the trial using STATA SE V. 12. The list was stratified by states (Haryana and Karnataka) and within states by availability/non-availability of nurses recruited under NPCDCS... Clusters (CHCs) within each strata were randomised to intervention or enhanced care arm using block randomisation (with a block size of 2)." Comment: This describes a proper random sequence generation process using a statistician, computer software (STATA), stratification, and block randomisation, which is sufficient to judge low risk for random sequence generation.

Summary: The paper reports that the randomisation list was generated by an independent statistician and that clusters were randomised within strata using block randomisation, but it does not describe any concealment mechanism for cluster allocation (e.g., central allocation or sealed opaque envelopes). Because no method is stated that would prevent foreknowledge of the upcoming assignment, allocation concealment cannot be judged as low risk.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Being a cluster randomised trial, only limited blinding could be enforced." and "In the intervention arm... nurse and physician are providing treatment and follow-up... using mWellcare system" जबकि "In the enhanced care arm, nurse and physicians were provided ‘refresher’ training..." Comment: Participants and clinic staff were not blinded to allocation; the intervention required visible changes in care delivery (tablet-based decision support, SMS reminders, extra follow-up), making knowledge of assignment likely and plausibly able to influence outcomes such as blood pressure, HbA1c, lifestyle counselling, and follow-up adherence.

Quote: "Endline assessment of outcomes will be undertaken after completion of 12-month follow-up. The assessments will be carried out by independent outcome assessors inviting the participants to camps and mop up will be organised through home visits." Comment: Outcome assessment was done by independent outcome assessors, which indicates the assessors were separate from the treating team. The primary outcomes (SBP and HbA1c) are objective clinical measurements/blood tests, so even if assessors were not explicitly described as blinded, measurement of these outcomes is unlikely to be influenced by knowledge of allocation.

Summary: The protocol states that the primary analyses will be intention-to-treat and that "Participants who withdrew consent for use of their data will not be included in any analyses," but it does not report actual follow-up numbers, per-arm attrition, or reasons for missing outcome data for the trial results. Although the planned loss to follow-up was 20% and endline assessment would include camp visits and mop-up home visits, the available text is a protocol/characteristics description rather than outcome results, so the extent and balance of missing data cannot be judged.
